rulings.Case Title: IN RE: 3032 16TH STREET, LLC DBA JERRY HOTEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Appoint Umpire; And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Petition To Appoint Umpire
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 21, 2023, line 1. Petitioner's Motion to Appoint Umpire is GRANTED. AIIC may provide the Court with a list of proposed umpires by March 28, 2023. The parties may review their lists and agree upon an umpire, and notify the Court by April 3, 2023. In the event the parties cannot agree on an umpire, the Court will select an umpire from AIIC and Petitioner's lists on April 3, 2023. Petitioner is to provide a proposed order for the Court to appoint an umpire by April 3, 2023. No opposition filed. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA DARK ET AL VS. EMILIE M CALHOUN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Serve Summons Within Three Years
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 21, 2023, line 2. Defendant's Motion to Dismiss for Failure to Serve Summons Within 3 Years is DENIED. Moving party agreed to accept service by e-mail on the last day for service. No conditions were imposed on such service. No notice was provided to Plaintiff regarding the perceived deficiency on the same day. While there might have been a misunderstanding as to the conditions for e-mail service (e.g. in lieu of personal service on a party/agent vs. in lieu of mail service) there is no dispute that the Summons and Complaint were served before the 3-years expired and shortly after the last settlement attempt failed. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To: (1) Form Interrogatories, Set One; (2) Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions

rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO REQUIRE THE CIVIL CLERK TO ENTER THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 21, 2023, line 7. Motion to Require the Civil Clerk to Enter the Default Judgment is DENIED. rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS CAMERON ET AL VS. BJORN BAKSTAND ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 21, 2023, line 12. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant is not entitled to judgment as a matter of law due to the existence of triable issues of material fact as to Plaintiffs' intent to occupy the premises for 36 months, and as to whether Plaintiff paid Defendants the statutorily mandated relocation expenses. (Code Civ. Proc., 437(c). Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE KWONG VS. BRADLEY R. BUCKLAND ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 21, 2023, line 9. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED. Plaintiff's notice and declaration of the unlawful detainer are not defective. Plaintiff properly describes the rental properties by providing a common street address of each property. (San Francisco Rent Ordinance section 37.9B(c)(4).) The declaration is not defective because Plaintiff included language in her declaration stating that she has not recovered possession of any other rental unit in San Francisco. (Rent Board Rule and Regulation 12.14(b)(8)(iv).) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/SKF)

rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK TY VS. PEDRO CASTENEDA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 21, 2023, line 10. Defendant's Motion to Vacate Judgment. Hearing required. By no later than 9:00 a.m. on March 21, 2023, Defendant to file, serve and present to the Court a courtesy copy of the proof of service of the March 14, 2023 Order Shortening Time on hearing on motion to vacate judgment. The Court is inclined to GRANT the motion, if the proof of timely service of the Order shortening time (at the same time as the motion) is filed with the Court. The Default Judgment will be vacated on grounds of mistake and excusable neglect. (Code Civ. Proc. 473(b).) No opposition filed. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JASON YUEN ET AL VS. ERENIA RAMOS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 21, 2023, line 11. Defendant's Demurrer to the Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to reconcile allegations regarding service of the notice and expiration of the notice. (The 3-Day Notice to Pay Rent or Quit was served on January 31, 2023. The Complaint alleges that the notice expired on February 6, 2023). No opposition filed.
